title: cheap
description: Eugene Volokh — Cheap Speech and What It Will Do
show_notes:
Eugene Volokh — Cheap Speech and What It Will Do

Eugene Volokh argues that emerging digital-distribution technologies — the "information superhighway" — will dramatically reduce the cost of producing and distributing speech across music, books, magazines, newspapers, and video, breaking the power of publisher-intermediaries and shifting control to speakers and listeners. The resulting "cheap speech" environment will democratize the information marketplace, giving fringe voices access while letting listeners custom-tailor their media diets — outcomes that resemble the idealized open marketplace assumed by existing First Amendment doctrine. Volokh acknowledges a possible dark side (KKK-adjacent speakers also benefit, and the loss of a common mass-media baseline may fray social cohesion), but concludes the First Amendment will fit the new order better than it fit the old.

2:06 I. focus
28:19 Introduction
1:38:11 I. may be wrong in my predictions about what the new media order will
